rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice For New Hearing Date Re Defendant Motion: To Vacate Pre-Trial Sanction That Resuscitated Abated & Closed Violations To Enable Awards Of Dbi Penalties & Attorney Fees
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 15, 2021 line 2. DEFENDANT ANNE KIHAGI Amended Notice For New Hearing Date Re Defendant Motion: To Vacate Pre-Trial Sanction That Resuscitated Abated & Closed Violations To Enable Awards Of Dbi Penalties & Attorney Fees is DENIED on substantive and procedural grounds. The issues of jurisdiction and discovery sanctions have been determined on appeal. Moving party failed to cite any case where a trial Court vacated its discovery order that had been affirmed on appeal or where a trial Court vacated its judgment as void for lack of jurisdiction after the judgment was affirmed on appeal to support its requested relief for the Court to "make an appropriate order vacating the sanction order as void, and appropriately reversing all appropriate parts of the judgment that arose from that order." (Points and Authorities 21:2-3). The Court notes that moving party's brief was 20 pages long. Going forward all oversized briefs and briefs filed in violation of any CCP or CRC provisions will be stricken in their entirety. Exhibit k-A to Kihagi Declaration is stricken. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Defendant Motion - For An Order Denying Receivers Request For Payments & An Order To Turn-Over All Accounts And Operations Of Receiver Pending Decisions On Appeal
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 15, 2021 line 2. DEFENDANT ANNE KIHAGI Notice Of Defendant Motion - For An Order Denying Receivers Request For Payments & An Order To Turn-Over All Accounts And Operations Of Receiver Pending Decisions On Appeal is DENIED. As this Court has previously explained on multiple occasions the appeal from orders denying Defendants motions to terminate/void/vacate, etc. the order appointing the receiver years after the receiver took over the estate does not create a stay because as far as those orders are concerned, there is nothing to stay. By those decisions the defendants had been denied the affirmative relief which they sought. The decisions required no process of the Court to carry them into effect. As the Court has previously stated on the record the ruling could have been different had defendant appealed the original order appointing the receiver before the received took over. At this time, and based on the procedural posture of the case, the request to order the receiver to turn over all accounts and operations is denied. The motion for order denying receiver request for payments is denied as procedurally improper. In the future Defendant shall follow procedures outlined in CRC 3.1183 to object to receiver's report and accounting. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Anne Kihagi'S Notice Of Motion To Set-Aside Rulings Of July 23, 2021, March 1, 2021 And April 7, 2021 Due To Judicial Bad Faith Under Supreme Court Authority In "Broadman" Decision
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 15, 2021 line 2. Defendant Anne Kihagi'S Notice Of Motion To Set-Aside Rulings Of July 23, 2021, March 1, 2021 And April 7, 2021 Due To Judicial Bad Faith Under Supreme Court Authority In "Broadman" Decision is DENIED. Authorities cited in support of this motion do not provide for procedural mechanism to vacate these orders at the trial level on the grounds stated in the motion. While it is possible that a trial judge may make erroneous rulings while carefully considering parties' respective briefs and arguments on issues unsupported by any precedent on point both procedurally and substantively, there are limited procedural mechanisms to address such errors at the trial level, e.g. motion for reconsideration, motion for a new trial, etc. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 15, 2021 line 4. CROSS DEFENDANT STELLA CHEN DEMURRER to 2ND Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is continued to January 14, 2022. On November 5, 2021 this Court issued a minute order directing Cross-Complainant's then counsel of record, Linda Z. Yu, to file a declaration addressing failure to comply with this Court's October 4, 2021 Order. Declaration was to be filed and served no later than November 18, 2021. Court's records do not reflect a filing of such declaration. No later than December 29, 2021, Linda Z. rulings.Case Title: 640 OCTAVIA, LLC VS. KARL HEINZ PIEPER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Motion For Summary Judgment Filed By 640 Octavia, Llc
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 15, 2021 line 6. PLAINTIFF 640 OCTAVIA, LLC Amended MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Motion For Summary Judgment Filed By 640 Octavia, LLC is GRANTED. Moving party shifted its burden and established all elements of Ellis Act unlawful detainer. Opposing party failed to create a triable issue as to any material fact regarding elements of the cause of action or affirmative defense. Plaintiff's objections to Defendants' evidence in support of opposition are sustained in part and overruled in part: 1. Overruled - 1, 2, 20-24 2. Overruled as to the hearsay objection: this is not an out-of-Court statement, evidence was admitted by the trial court and affirmed on appeal; and not a statement by a person under Cal. Evid. Code 225(b) as made by a recording device. 3. Sustained as irrelevant -3-17, 19, 25-29 4. Sustained: speculative opinion - 10 5. Sustained: lack of foundation =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
